full_prompt: Design a lightweight structural bracket for a CubeSat secondary-payload adapter per a representative lightweight-structural-bracket brief: the bracket connects a 6U CubeSat mass model (nominal 12 kg) to an ESPA-class secondary payload ring, meeting NASA-STD-5001B safety factors (1.4 ultimate, 1.25 yield), NASA GEVS random-vibration qualification (14.1 g RMS, 20-2000 Hz), and a mass-reduction goal of >=40% vs a machined-billet baseline. Envelope: 150 x 150 x 100 mm, with an ICD-fixed bolt pattern on the ESPA interface (four M8 bolts on a 110 x 110 mm square pattern) and a CubeSat-interface pattern on the opposite face (four M6 bolts on a 90 x 90 mm pattern, 100 x 100 mm outer). Material: Ti-6Al-4V ELI (Grade 23, Young's modulus 113.8 GPa, Poisson 0.342, density 4430 kg/m^3, yield 795 MPa, UTS 860 MPa, minimum elongation 10%) hot-isostatic-pressed AM or wrought; AM route must be post-processed by HIP to close internal porosity. Load cases from NASA LSP-REQ-317.01 Rev B quasi-static + NASA GEVS: LC1 quasi-static 12.5 g axial along launch direction with payload mass 12 kg = 1,471 N; LC2 quasi-static 8 g lateral = 941 N; LC3 sine sweep 2 g 0-peak 5-50 Hz; LC4 random vibration 14.1 g RMS 20-2000 Hz input at ESPA interface, with Miles' equation response magnification or full PSD analysis; LC5 thermal-gradient induced stress between +80 C (sun) and -100 C (eclipse) computed with alpha_Ti = 9.7e-6 /K. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises under any quasi-static or sine case <= 860 / 1.4 = 614 MPa (NASA-STD-5001B ultimate); (R2) max von Mises <= 795 / 1.25 = 636 MPa (yield); the binding constraint is R1 at 614 MPa since 614 < 636; (R3) random-vibration 3-sigma von Mises <= 614 MPa (same ultimate gate applied on 3-sigma statistical bound); (R4) first natural frequency >= 100 Hz (NASA launch-coupled-load decoupling typical for CubeSat secondary) and >= 50 Hz (ESPA requirement floor, looser); binding is 100 Hz; (R5) bracket mass <= 300 g AND at least 40% lighter than a machined-billet baseline filling the 150x150x100 mm envelope; (R6) thermal-gradient induced max von Mises <= 614 MPa at delta-T = 180 C (worst sun-to-eclipse swing); (R7) AM manufacturability: minimum feature 0.5 mm, overhang >= 45 deg, internal void <= 8 mm for powder removal, mandatory post-HIP at 920 C for 2 hours. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + 3MF of the final part; per-load-case FEA report with quasi-static, sine, random, and thermal stresses; modal analysis first-mode frequency; mass and baseline-comparison report; AM build orientation and HIP schedule.
short_prompt:
Design a lightweight structural bracket connecting a 6U CubeSat mass model (nominal 12 kg) to an ESPA-class secondary payload ring, meeting NASA-STD-5001B safety factors (1.4 ultimate, 1.25 yield), NASA GEVS random vibration (14.1 g RMS, 20-2000 Hz), and a mass-reduction goal of >= 40% vs a machined-billet baseline.

Envelope: 150 x 150 x 100 mm. ESPA interface: four M8 bolts on 110 x 110 mm. CubeSat interface: four M6 bolts on 90 x 90 mm (100 x 100 mm outer).

Material: Ti-6Al-4V ELI (Grade 23), wrought or HIP'd AM. AM route post-processed by HIP at 920 C for 2 hours.

Load cases per NASA LSP-REQ-317.01 Rev B + NASA GEVS.
- LC1 quasi-static 12.5 g axial with 12 kg payload = 1,471 N.
- LC2 quasi-static 8 g lateral = 941 N.
- LC3 sine sweep 2 g 0-peak 5-50 Hz.
- LC4 random vibration 14.1 g RMS 20-2000 Hz at the ESPA interface.
- LC5 thermal gradient +80 C (sun) to -100 C (eclipse) with alpha_Ti = 9.7e-6/K.

Gates include first natural mode >= 100 Hz, mass <= 300 g, AM manufacturability (min feature 0.5 mm, overhang >= 45 deg, internal void <= 8 mm).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 40% mass reduction target drives topo-opt outer loop.
Closed-form Miles response if PSD FEA not run.

prompt.title: Ti-6Al-4V ELI CubeSat-to-ESPA secondary bracket per lightweight-structural-bracket brief
prompt.brief: Design a 150x150x100 mm Ti-6Al-4V ELI bracket connecting a 12 kg 6U CubeSat to an ESPA ring, meeting NASA-STD-5001B, GEVS random, thermal gradient, and 40% mass reduction.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: NASA launch-coupled-load decoupling for CubeSat secondary
feature >=0.5 mm, overhang >=45 deg, HIP post-process

requirements.governing_standard: NASA-STD-5001B + NASA-STD-7001B (GEVS) + NASA LSP-REQ-317.01 Rev B

exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Radiation dose is a separate component selection exercise, not structural.

notes.numeric_source: NASA-STD-5001B, NASA-STD-7001B GEVS 14.1 g RMS, NASA LSP-REQ-317.01 Rev B quasi-static envelope, Ti-6Al-4V ELI mechanical data.
